change.description: Compact each original c8 rowchunk2 pair into standalone c2 fixtures, compare native full-attention O against forced batch-GEMV O.

findings: Standalone c2 native-full-attention O is green only for original rows0..1; rows2..3, rows4..5, and rows6..7 reproduce the same red prefixes as the c8 rowchunk2 forced-native-O chunks: [137,118], [45,137], and [68,137].
Forcing the batch-GEMV full-attention O path makes every compact c2 pair generated-token green at [137,137] with empty mismatch summaries.

Therefore the c8 rowchunk2 native-O frontier is not a rowchunk-slice/copy-only issue. The native rows=2 full-attention O projection itself is prompt-pair sensitive, and row-aware batch-GEMV O is the correctness repair even for c2 prompt pairs beyond the leading fixture rows.

guard.command: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 -m compileall -q hipengine tests scripts && HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 pytest -q tests/test_generation_batch_scheduler.py tests/test_generation_qwen35_paro.py tests/test_qwen35_resident_batch_layout.py tests/test_kvcache_policy.py tests/test_kvcache_spans.py tests/test_server_api.py -q && HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_correctness.py --rows 2 --json /tmp/hipengine-e2e-primitive-c2.json && HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_correctness.py --rows 8 --json /tmp/hipengine-e2e-primitive-c8.json

prompt_verifier.evidence: Pass: this makes the focused full-attention O blocker green for all compact c2 prompt pairs using batch-GEMV O ([137,137] each), while native O red pairs identify the failing component. No throughput claim.
